name: LIHEAP & Universal Service Fund (Energy Assistance)
org: NJ Dept. of Community Affairs / DCAid
summary: Helps low-income households pay heating and cooling bills and can stop a utility shutoff. USF lowers your monthly electric and gas bill year-round; LIHEAP gives a seasonal benefit toward what you owe.
services: Help paying electric and gas bills
Emergency assistance to prevent or restore a shutoff

eligibility.summary: Low-to-moderate income NJ households; you do not need to be behind on the bill to apply.
eligibility.income: Household income at or below ~60% of state median income (about $5,800/mo for a family of 3).
eligibility.residency: New Jersey resident; responsible for home heating/cooling costs.
eligibility.household: All household sizes; benefit scales with size.
eligibility.notes: Renters whose utilities are included in rent may still qualify. Immigration status of the applicant's children does not affect a citizen child's eligibility.

requiredDocs: Most recent electric and gas bills
Proof of income for the last 4 weeks (pay stubs, benefit letters)
Proof of address (lease or recent mail)
Social Security numbers or ITINs for household members who have them

location.address: Apply online via DCAid; in-person help at Hudson County Community Action

source: Synthetic record modeled on NJ LIHEAP/USF public program info

name: SNAP (Food Assistance / NJ SNAP)
org: Hudson County Board of Social Services
summary: Monthly money on a card to buy groceries. The amount depends on your household size and income, and benefits can start within days in an emergency.
services: Monthly grocery benefits on an EBT card
Expedited (emergency) SNAP within 7 days for very low income

eligibility.summary: Low-income households; most families with reduced hours or recent job loss qualify.
eligibility.income: Gross monthly income at or below 185% of the federal poverty level (about $4,000/mo for a family of 3).
eligibility.residency: Must live in Hudson County, NJ.
eligibility.household: Counts everyone who buys and prepares food together.
eligibility.notes: A U.S.-born or qualified-immigrant child can receive SNAP even if a parent is ineligible.

requiredDocs: Proof of income for the last 30 days (or proof of job loss / reduced hours)
Social Security numbers for members applying
Recent rent and utility bills (for the deduction that can raise your benefit)
source: Synthetic record modeled on NJ SNAP public program info

name: WIC (Women, Infants & Children Nutrition)
summary: Free healthy food, formula, and nutrition support for pregnant people and kids under 5. Marisol's 5-year-old may still qualify until their 5th birthday.
services: Monthly benefits for milk, eggs, produce, formula, and cereal

eligibility.summary: Pregnant, postpartum, or breastfeeding people and children under age 5 with low-to-moderate income.
eligibility.income: Household income at or below 185% of the federal poverty level; being on SNAP or Medicaid qualifies you automatically.
eligibility.household: Each eligible child and parent counts.
eligibility.notes: Open to families regardless of immigration status; using WIC does not affect immigration cases.

requiredDocs: The child's birth certificate or medical record
Proof of income or a SNAP/Medicaid eligibility letter
source: Synthetic record modeled on NJ WIC public program info

name: York Street Project Food Pantry
summary: A neighborhood food pantry where you can pick up free groceries the same day, no appointment needed. Good for filling the fridge this week while longer applications process.

eligibility.summary: Open to anyone in need in the Jersey City area; no income paperwork required to receive food.
eligibility.residency: Walk-ins welcome; serves Hudson County residents.
eligibility.notes: No ID or immigration status required to receive groceries.

requiredDocs: Proof of address helpful but optional
source: Synthetic record modeled on a Jersey City food pantry

name: NJ SHARES Emergency Utility Grant
summary: A one-time grant to help households that are slightly over the income limit for LIHEAP but still can't cover an electric or gas bill and face a shutoff.
services: One-time grant paid directly to the utility
Help for households just above other program limits

eligibility.summary: Households experiencing a temporary financial hardship who have made a good-faith payment effort.
eligibility.income: Generally for low-to-moderate income households above the LIHEAP cutoff.
eligibility.residency: New Jersey resident with a utility account in their name.
eligibility.notes: Usually requires a recent payment toward the balance.

requiredDocs: The overdue utility bill or shutoff notice
Proof of a recent payment toward the bill
source: Synthetic record modeled on NJ SHARES public program info

name: WorkFirst NJ / TANF (Temporary Cash Assistance)
org: Hudson County Board of Social Services
summary: Short-term monthly cash for families with children who have little or no income, plus help finding work and emergency assistance for rent or utilities.
services: Monthly cash assistance for families with children
Emergency Assistance for rent or utility crises

eligibility.summary: Families with children and very low income and resources.
eligibility.income: Very low income; specific limits depend on household size.
eligibility.notes: Time-limited; comes with work requirements once stabilized.

requiredDocs: Social Security numbers for household members
source: Synthetic record modeled on WorkFirst NJ public program info

name: NJ Child Care Subsidy
org: NJ Division of Family Development / Hudson County
summary: Helps working or job-searching parents pay for child care so they can keep or look for a job. Covers part of the cost at approved providers.
services: Help paying for licensed child care
Coverage while working, training, or job searching

eligibility.summary: Working or job-searching parents with low-to-moderate income and children under 13.
eligibility.income: At or below 200% of the federal poverty level at application.
eligibility.household: Children under 13 (or older with special needs).
eligibility.notes: A recent job loss with active job search can still qualify you.

requiredDocs: Proof of income or job-search activity
source: Synthetic record modeled on NJ Child Care Subsidy public info

name: Emergency Rental & Eviction Prevention Assistance
summary: Help for renters at risk of falling behind or facing eviction — short-term rental help and connection to legal and housing counseling.

eligibility.summary: Renters with low income who are behind or at risk of falling behind on rent.
eligibility.income: Low-to-moderate income; priority for households facing imminent loss of housing.
eligibility.notes: Not a legal determination — staff help you understand options, not decide your case.

requiredDocs: Any late-rent or eviction notice received
Proof of income and the hardship (e.g., reduced hours letter)
source: Synthetic record modeled on county housing assistance

name: Northeast NJ Legal Services
summary: Free legal help for low-income residents on utility shutoffs, evictions, and denied benefits. They can talk to your utility company or appear in housing court for you.
services: Appeals when a benefit is wrongly denied

eligibility.summary: Low-income residents of Hudson, Bergen, Essex, or Hudson-area counties.
eligibility.income: Generally at or below 200% of the federal poverty level.
eligibility.residency: Serves northeastern NJ counties including Hudson and Bergen.
eligibility.notes: Provides legal advice and representation — Compass does not.

requiredDocs: Any court papers, shutoff notice, or denial letter
source: Synthetic record modeled on Legal Services of NJ

name: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
summary: Free, confidential support 24/7 for anyone in emotional distress or crisis — for you or someone you care about. Call or text any time.
services: 24/7 crisis counseling by call or text
Support for emotional distress, not only suicide

eligibility.summary: Anyone, any time. No eligibility requirements.
eligibility.notes: If someone is in immediate danger, call 911.

source: Synthetic record modeled on the 988 Lifeline

name: SNAP & Temporary Assistance (Bergen County)
org: Bergen County Board of Social Services
summary: Bergen County's office for food assistance (SNAP) and temporary cash help for residents who live in Bergen rather than Hudson County.

eligibility.income: Gross income at or below 185% of the federal poverty level for SNAP.
eligibility.residency: Must live in Bergen County, NJ.
eligibility.notes: Apply here only if you live in Bergen County; Hudson residents use the Hudson office.

requiredDocs: Proof of income for the last 30 days
source: Synthetic record modeled on Bergen County BSS public info

name: Center for Food Action Pantry (Bergen)
summary: Emergency groceries and help with rent and utility emergencies for Bergen County families, with same-day food available.
services: Help with one-time rent or utility emergencies

eligibility.summary: Bergen County residents experiencing a food or financial emergency.
eligibility.residency: Serves Bergen County and nearby towns.
eligibility.notes: No immigration status required for food assistance.

requiredDocs: Proof of address for food help
For rent/utility help: the bill or notice and proof of income
source: Synthetic record modeled on a Bergen County food agency
